_comment: Engaged-soldier highlight demo (Settings.show_engaged_highlight, dev/debug visual): a fresh scenario authored for this specific demo, not a copy of anti-cav-square.json's setup -- see .claude/memories/sparta-demos.md's 'author each demo scenario fresh' entry. A single player Spearmen unit is pressed from THREE directions at once (front-left, front-right, and rear) rather than the two-rear-attacker case anti-cav-square.json shows, so the clip also demonstrates the omnidirectional multi-attacker pressure #752 itself is about. Selects the unit, holds it in place (Ctrl+H -- suppresses the auto-chase toward a detected enemy, so the block receives the charge stationary instead of drifting to meet it; still fights normally once contact is made), then squares up (O). Requests the highlight via the session-only 'show_engaged_highlight' script field (Settings.set_show_engaged_highlight_session, DemoInputRecorder.gd), never persisted. Timing verified locally via dump-state.sh: casualties (soldier count dropping) begin around tick 200 and continue through the end of the clip, so the amber highlight is shown tracking the LIVE ring as the per-soldier array compacts -- the exact case UnitFormation.live_perimeter_indices fixes.
